div1.entryFree: Pertaunt-like (Ff Q2) or Perttaunt-like (Q1) a word not yet explained nor satisfactorily amended in LLL V, 2, 67: so p. would I o'ersway his state that he should be my fool and I his fate. Theobald pedant-like, Hanmer portent-like, Singer potent-like, Collier M. C. potently, Grant White persaunt-like.*
